name: Circular walk through Tudela
region_intro: Hiking around Tudela offers diverse landscapes, from the semi-desert terrain of Bardenas Reales Natural Park to the serene riverside paths along the Ebro River. The region is characterized by unique eroded rock formations, extensive green corridors, and wetlands. These varied environments provide a range of hiking experiences, from easy strolls to moderate ascents. Best hiking trails around Tudela The most popular hiking route is Circular walk through Tudela, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, exploring the city's surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ebro Green Corridor from Tudela, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.4 km) path. This route follows the Ebro River, offering views of the natural corridor. Local hikers also love the Balsa de Pulguer Loop, a 1.9 miles (3.0 km) trail leading through a saline wetland, often completed in about 46 minutes. Hiking around Tudela is defined by semi-desert formations, riverside paths, and wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Tudela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 270 hikers have used komoot to explore Tudela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Tudela?
A: The hiking trails around Tudela offer a diverse range of terrain. You can explore the unique semi-desert landscapes of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, characterized by eroded rock formations and plateaus. Alternatively, you'll find serene, flat paths along the Ebro River and its green corridor, as well as wetland areas. For those seeking more elevation, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379816738/canraso-heights-and-ebro-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canraso Heights and Ebro River Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer moderate ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Tudela?
A: Yes, Tudela offers several easy and family-friendly hiking options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379826809/balsa-de-pulguer-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Pulguer Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy trail through a saline wetland, perfect for birdwatching and a relaxed stroll. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379792465/circular-walk-through-tudela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through Tudela&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the city's surroundings on an easy 6.7 km path.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see on hikes near Tudela?
A: The region boasts several impressive natural landmarks and viewpoints. The most iconic is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, a striking eroded pinnacle within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. Other areas like El Rallón and Piskerra within the park also offer breathtaking panoramic views of the unique semi-desert landscape. Along the Ebro River, you can enjoy views of the river itself and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1389734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bocal Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Tudela?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379792465/circular-walk-through-tudela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through Tudela&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option. For a slightly longer, moderate experience with more elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379816738/canraso-heights-and-ebro-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canraso Heights and Ebro River Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379826809/balsa-de-pulguer-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Pulguer Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers an easy circular path through a wetland.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Tudela?
A: The best time for hiking in Tudela is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summer can be very hot, especially in the Bardenas Reales, while winter can bring colder temperatures, though hiking is still possible.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in the Tudela area?
A: There are approximately 48 hiking trails available around Tudela, offering a wide range of experiences. About half of these are considered easy, while the other half are moderate, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tudela?
A: The trails in Tudela are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the unique semi-desert formations to the peaceful riverside paths, and appreciate the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through Tudela?
A: Yes, Tudela is a point along significant long-distance trails. It is part of the Ignatian Way, a pilgrimage route, and also lies on the Natural Route of the Ebro (GR 99), which traces the Ebro river valley for over 950 km.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails near Tudela using public transport?
A: While Tudela itself is well-connected, direct public transport to some specific trailheads, especially within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting Tudela to nearby villages or park entrances. For exploring the Bardenas Reales, guided 4x4 or buggy tours are also available, or you may consider driving.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions to see while hiking near Tudela?
A: Yes, the region blends nature with history. Along the Ebro Green Corridor, you can explore historical sites like the Presa del Bocal and the Imperial Canal of Aragón. Within the Bardenas Reales, the Vedado de Eguarás features the ruins of a medieval castle, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4766416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Santa María de Tudela&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5784182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Fueros de Tudela&lt;/a&gt; are also notable attractions within the city itself.

Q: Are permits required for hiking in the Bardenas Reales Natural Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for hiking on the marked tracks and paths within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. However, it's always recommended to check the official park regulations before your visit, especially for any specific restricted areas or seasonal closures. For general information on hiking in the region, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/hiking" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for birdwatching around Tudela?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379826809/balsa-de-pulguer-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Pulguer Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for birdwatching. This saline wetland is a Special Area of Conservation and provides a habitat for various bird species, making it a rewarding spot for nature enthusiasts.
tips:
The Ebro Bridge in Tudela is an impressive work of medieval architecture. Built of stone, it is 360 meters long and consists of 16 piers and 17 unequal arches. This bridge is fundamental to the history of Tudela, as it has been a strategic crossing over the Ebro River since its construction in the 12th and 13th centuries.

Although tradition attributes its construction to King Sancho VII the Strong, some historians suggest it could be a reconstruction of an earlier Muslim bridge. Over the years, it has undergone multiple renovations to withstand floods and the passage of time.

Tudela is a charming city with much to offer!
Tudela Cathedral: A stunning example of Gothic and Romanesque architecture, with its famous Judgment Gate.
Plaza de los Fueros: The heart of the city, ideal for strolling and soaking up the local atmosphere.
Muñoz Sola Museum of Modern Art: Perfect for art lovers, with a collection of 19th-century works.
Bridge over the Ebro River: A picturesque spot to enjoy views of the river.
Also, don't miss the opportunity to try the delicious local cuisine, especially the fresh vegetables from Tudela's gardens.
A city map hangs on the wall of a house in the Plaza de Los Fueros. A path is shown there as a red line. We used this as a guide to stroll through this interesting old town.
The Tudela Food Market is an iconic location in the city of Tudela, Navarre. Founded in 1841, this market is known for offering a wide variety of fresh, high-quality local produce.
The paintings on the façade are surprising.
One more church in this beautiful city.
It seems incredible that this beautiful church can be found a stone's throw from the imposing cathedral.

The Monument to the Heart of Jesus in Tudela is an impressive statue located atop the Cerro del Castillo. It was inaugurated on October 25, 1942, the feast of Christ the King, at the initiative of Father Luis Ortiz. The 12-meter-high statue was created by the Italian sculptor Carlo Buzzi, and the design was carried out by the architect Víctor Eusa.

The monument stands as a spiritual beacon that extends its protection over the city and the surrounding area. From its location, one can enjoy a beautiful panoramic view of Tudela, the Ebro River, and its surroundings. Furthermore, the monument's surroundings are landscaped like a park, with remains of the old castle, walls, cisterns, and passageways.

The Church of the Company of Mary, also known as the Church of La Enseñanza, is one of the finest examples of Baroque architecture in Tudela. Built between 1732 and 1742, this church stands out for its octagonal design and lantern dome.
Located on Plaza de San Juan and Calle de la Enseñanza, the church is part of a complex that includes a convent and a school, both of which were later renovated.

The Paseo de los Poetas in Tudela is a lovely place to enjoy a peaceful stroll. This promenade is located next to the Queiles River and features a picturesque bridge that connects the green areas of the promenade. It's an ideal place to relax, enjoy nature, and exercise, as it also features benches, swings, and bio-healthy exercise equipment.

The Monreal Tower is a historic defensive tower located in Tudela. Originally built in the 13th century, its current appearance is due to several subsequent reconstructions. The tower has a hexagonal shape and is made of brick, integrating an ancient medieval cistern.

Today, the Monreal Tower houses Navarre's first camera obscura, a device that projects real-time images of the tower's surroundings. It also has an interpretation center with explanatory panels about the Jewish, Muslim, and Christian cultures that have coexisted in Tudela.

The Monument to the Heart of Mary in Tudela is an impressive statue dedicated to the Virgin Mary, located on a hill in front of the Monreal Tower, near the Lourdes neighborhood. It was inaugurated on October 7, 1956, after the foundation stone was laid on December 8, 1954.

The statue rests on a slender, truncated-cone obelisk, reaching a total height of 21.5 meters.
Pleasant square near the Ebro river car park.
Nice walk next to the river.
